claim: The Helios mempool orders pending transactions by a priority score combining the offered fee rate and how long the transaction has waited, rather than by fee rate alone.

supporting_evidence.quote: The Helios mempool orders pending transactions by a priority score combining the offered fee rate and how long the transaction has waited, rather than by fee rate alone.

claim: This hybrid scoring prevents a purely fee-based mempool from indefinitely starving low-fee transactions during a sustained period of high demand.

supporting_evidence.quote: This hybrid scoring prevents a purely fee-based mempool from indefinitely starving low-fee transactions during a sustained period of high demand.

claim: A sender wishing to speed up a stuck transaction can broadcast a replacement carrying an identical set of inputs but a strictly higher fee, a mechanism the network calls replace-by-fee.

supporting_evidence.quote: A sender wishing to speed up a stuck transaction can broadcast a replacement carrying an identical set of inputs but a strictly higher fee, a mechanism the network calls replace-by-fee.

claim: Nodes accept a replacement only if its fee rate exceeds the original by at least a minimum increment, preventing an attacker from cheaply spamming trivial fee bumps to churn mempool state.

supporting_evidence.quote: Nodes accept a replacement only if its fee rate exceeds the original by at least a minimum increment, preventing an attacker from cheaply spamming trivial fee bumps to churn mempool state.

claim: Helios evicts the lowest-priority transaction currently held whenever a higher-priority transaction arrives and the mempool is already at capacity.

supporting_evidence.quote: Helios evicts the lowest-priority transaction currently held whenever a higher-priority transaction arrives and the mempool is already at capacity.

claim: Transactions that have waited beyond a configurable maximum age are evicted regardless of their fee rate, preventing extremely old, likely-stale transactions from occupying space indefinitely.

supporting_evidence.quote: Transactions that have waited beyond a configurable maximum age are evicted regardless of their fee rate, preventing extremely old, likely-stale transactions from occupying space indefinitely.

claim: Helios propagates transactions using a push-based gossip protocol: a node forwards a newly seen transaction to a random subset of its peers rather than waiting to be asked for it.

supporting_evidence.quote: Helios propagates transactions using a push-based gossip protocol: a node forwards a newly seen transaction to a random subset of its peers rather than waiting to be asked for it.

claim: A transaction typically reaches ninety-five percent of Helios mempool-tracking nodes within four seconds under normal network conditions.

supporting_evidence.quote: A transaction typically reaches ninety-five percent of Helios mempool-tracking nodes within four seconds under normal network conditions.

claim: Helios requires every mempool-accepted transaction to pay at least a minimum relay fee, a floor well below typical market rates but high enough to make bulk spam economically costly.

supporting_evidence.quote: Helios requires every mempool-accepted transaction to pay at least a minimum relay fee, a floor well below typical market rates but high enough to make bulk spam economically costly.

claim: Nodes also rate-limit how many transactions they will accept from a single peer within a short window, independent of the fees those transactions carry.

supporting_evidence.quote: Nodes also rate-limit how many transactions they will accept from a single peer within a short window, independent of the fees those transactions carry.

claim: Because dependent transactions must be included in order, the queue also tracks a small dependency graph so that a child transaction is never selected before its parent.

supporting_evidence.quote: Because dependent transactions must be included in order, the queue also tracks a small dependency graph so that a child transaction is never selected before its parent.

claim: Producers running the reference implementation report the priority queue operations account for well under one percent of total block-assembly time even at peak mempool occupancy.

supporting_evidence.quote: Producers running the reference implementation report the priority queue operations account for well under one percent of total block-assembly time even at peak mempool occupancy.

claim: Several wallets now implement automatic replace-by-fee, silently rebroadcasting a stuck transaction at a higher fee after a configurable delay without further user interaction.

supporting_evidence.quote: Several wallets now implement automatic replace-by-fee, silently rebroadcasting a stuck transaction at a higher fee after a configurable delay without further user interaction.

claim: This automation has measurably reduced support requests related to stuck transactions, according to feedback several wallet teams have shared with the Helios core development mailing list.

supporting_evidence.quote: This automation has measurably reduced support requests related to stuck transactions, according to feedback several wallet teams have shared with the Helios core development mailing list.

claim: The Helios reference client ships with a default mempool memory budget of 300 megabytes, sufficient to hold roughly 150,000 typical transactions simultaneously.

supporting_evidence.quote: The Helios reference client ships with a default mempool memory budget of 300 megabytes, sufficient to hold roughly 150,000 typical transactions simultaneously.

claim: During the network's busiest recorded period, in October 2022, mempool occupancy across tracked nodes averaged 96 percent of capacity for nearly eighteen hours.

supporting_evidence.quote: During the network's busiest recorded period, in October 2022, mempool occupancy across tracked nodes averaged 96 percent of capacity for nearly eighteen hours.

claim: Median time from broadcast to block inclusion during that period rose to 340 seconds, compared to a typical baseline of under 20 seconds.

supporting_evidence.quote: Median time from broadcast to block inclusion during that period rose to 340 seconds, compared to a typical baseline of under 20 seconds.

claim: The minimum relay fee is the floor every mempool-accepted transaction must clear, set deliberately low enough to avoid blocking legitimate low-value transfers.

supporting_evidence.quote: The minimum relay fee is the floor every mempool-accepted transaction must clear, set deliberately low enough to avoid blocking legitimate low-value transfers.

claim: Helios has maintained a minimum relay fee of 1,000 fee units since the network's mempool specification was first ratified, unchanged across every subsequent client release.

supporting_evidence.quote: Helios has maintained a minimum relay fee of 1,000 fee units since the network's mempool specification was first ratified, unchanged across every subsequent client release.

claim: That floor is set well below the market fee rate observed even during the network's quietest periods, so it functions purely as an anti-spam measure rather than a meaningful cost for ordinary users.

supporting_evidence.quote: That floor is set well below the market fee rate observed even during the network's quietest periods, so it functions purely as an anti-spam measure rather than a meaningful cost for ordinary users.

claim: Sable, a zk-rollup network built on a separate settlement chain, operates its own sequencer-side mempool with design choices distinct from a typical base-layer chain like Helios.

supporting_evidence.quote: Sable, a zk-rollup network built on a separate settlement chain, operates its own sequencer-side mempool with design choices distinct from a typical base-layer chain like Helios.

claim: Sable's mainnet launched in 2020, and its mempool has used a strict first-in-first-out ordering since launch, deliberately avoiding fee-based prioritization entirely.

supporting_evidence.quote: Sable's mainnet launched in 2020, and its mempool has used a strict first-in-first-out ordering since launch, deliberately avoiding fee-based prioritization entirely.

claim: This FIFO design was a deliberate choice to reduce the risk of transaction-ordering manipulation by the sequencer operator, at the cost of no fee-based expediting option for users.

supporting_evidence.quote: This FIFO design was a deliberate choice to reduce the risk of transaction-ordering manipulation by the sequencer operator, at the cost of no fee-based expediting option for users.

claim: Eviction thresholds vary by transaction age tier, giving newer transactions more room to wait for a fee increase before being dropped.

supporting_evidence.quote: Eviction thresholds vary by transaction age tier, giving newer transactions more room to wait for a fee increase before being dropped.

claim: A transaction that fails to clear its tier's fee-rate threshold before the tier's wait limit expires is evicted regardless of how much mempool capacity remains free at that moment.

supporting_evidence.quote: A transaction that fails to clear its tier's fee-rate threshold before the tier's wait limit expires is evicted regardless of how much mempool capacity remains free at that moment.

claim: Propagation latency depends on how well-connected a node is, as measured across the reference client's telemetry.

supporting_evidence.quote: Propagation latency depends on how well-connected a node is, as measured across the reference client's telemetry.

claim: Diminishing returns set in past roughly 32 peers, which is why the reference client's default peer count target sits in that range rather than higher.

supporting_evidence.quote: Diminishing returns set in past roughly 32 peers, which is why the reference client's default peer count target sits in that range rather than higher.

claim: A node applies the following checks, in order, before admitting a newly received transaction into its local mempool.

supporting_evidence.quote: A node applies the following checks, in order, before admitting a newly received transaction into its local mempool.

claim: A transaction failing any single check is rejected outright and never gossiped further, regardless of how it performs on the remaining checks.

supporting_evidence.quote: A transaction failing any single check is rejected outright and never gossiped further, regardless of how it performs on the remaining checks.

claim: A proposed replacement transaction must satisfy every rule below before a node will evict the original in its favor.

supporting_evidence.quote: A proposed replacement transaction must satisfy every rule below before a node will evict the original in its favor.

claim: The cap on total replacements exists specifically to bound the network bandwidth a single stuck transaction can consume through repeated fee-bump rebroadcasts.

supporting_evidence.quote: The cap on total replacements exists specifically to bound the network bandwidth a single stuck transaction can consume through repeated fee-bump rebroadcasts.

claim: Kobayashi and Sato's 手数料市場における公平な取引順序付け (2018) analyzes fairness properties of fee-based transaction ordering that directly informed Helios's hybrid fee-and-age scoring.

supporting_evidence.quote: Kobayashi and Sato's 手数料市場における公平な取引順序付け (2018) analyzes fairness properties of fee-based transaction ordering that directly informed Helios's hybrid fee-and-age scoring.

claim: Ivanova's Устойчивость к спам-атакам в распределённых мемпулах (2019) formalizes the rate-limiting defense Helios's DoS protections are modeled on.

supporting_evidence.quote: Ivanova's Устойчивость к спам-атакам в распределённых мемпулах (2019) formalizes the rate-limiting defense Helios's DoS protections are modeled on.

claim: Russian-language papers frequently distinguish комиссия (an ordinary fee) from приоритетный сбор (a priority surcharge), a distinction Helios's own hybrid model maps onto fairly directly.

supporting_evidence.quote: Russian-language papers frequently distinguish комиссия (an ordinary fee) from приоритетный сбор (a priority surcharge), a distinction Helios's own hybrid model maps onto fairly directly.

claim: German-language documentation for several European deployments uses Vorrangsgebühr (priority fee) specifically for the age-weighted component of a hybrid score like Helios's.

supporting_evidence.quote: German-language documentation for several European deployments uses Vorrangsgebühr (priority fee) specifically for the age-weighted component of a hybrid score like Helios's.

claim: Formally, score = alpha * (fee_rate / base_fee_rate) + (1 - alpha) * min(wait_seconds / max_wait_seconds, 1), where alpha is a tunable weight between zero and one.

supporting_evidence.quote: Formally, score = alpha * (fee_rate / base_fee_rate) + (1 - alpha) * min(wait_seconds / max_wait_seconds, 1), where alpha is a tunable weight between zero and one.

claim: The Helios reference client currently ships with alpha = 0.7, favoring fee rate but still giving long-waiting transactions a meaningfully growing score over time.

supporting_evidence.quote: The Helios reference client currently ships with alpha = 0.7, favoring fee rate but still giving long-waiting transactions a meaningfully growing score over time.

claim: Under a simplifying assumption that arrival priority is uniformly distributed relative to existing transactions, the probability the lowest-ranked transaction is evicted on any single arrival is 1/k.

supporting_evidence.quote: Under a simplifying assumption that arrival priority is uniformly distributed relative to existing transactions, the probability the lowest-ranked transaction is evicted on any single arrival is 1/k.

claim: Empirically measured eviction rates during congestion are noticeably lower than this uniform-arrival bound predicts, consistent with the age component's smoothing effect.

supporting_evidence.quote: Empirically measured eviction rates during congestion are noticeably lower than this uniform-arrival bound predicts, consistent with the age component's smoothing effect.

claim: The first real stress test came in late 2018, when a popular token launch drove a sustained surge in transaction volume far beyond anything the network had previously experienced.

supporting_evidence.quote: The first real stress test came in late 2018, when a popular token launch drove a sustained surge in transaction volume far beyond anything the network had previously experienced.

claim: This starvation risk led the team to the hybrid design actually shipped: a score blending fee rate with waiting time, so that a transaction's priority climbs the longer it waits, eventually clearing regardless of how many new higher-fee transactions arrive afterward.

supporting_evidence.quote: This starvation risk led the team to the hybrid design actually shipped: a score blending fee rate with waiting time, so that a transaction's priority climbs the longer it waits, eventually clearing regardless of how many new higher-fee transactions arrive afterward.

claim: Looking back, the core team describes the FIFO-to-hybrid transition as the single most consequential mempool change in the network's history, and the one most directly driven by real user-reported pain rather than a theoretical concern raised in advance.

supporting_evidence.quote: Looking back, the core team describes the FIFO-to-hybrid transition as the single most consequential mempool change in the network's history, and the one most directly driven by real user-reported pain rather than a theoretical concern raised in advance.

claim: Beginning on October 3rd, 2022, Helios experienced its most sustained mempool congestion event to date, triggered by a combination of a popular new application launch and an unrelated, coincidental surge in ordinary transfer volume.

supporting_evidence.quote: Beginning on October 3rd, 2022, Helios experienced its most sustained mempool congestion event to date, triggered by a combination of a popular new application launch and an unrelated, coincidental surge in ordinary transfer volume.

claim: Median time from broadcast to inclusion rose from a typical baseline under twenty seconds to a peak of roughly 340 seconds, and fee rates rose to nine times the trailing thirty-day median.

supporting_evidence.quote: Median time from broadcast to inclusion rose from a typical baseline under twenty seconds to a peak of roughly 340 seconds, and fee rates rose to nine times the trailing thirty-day median.

claim: The core development team's primary post-incident action was not a protocol change but a recommendation: wallet developers without replace-by-fee support were strongly encouraged to add it, given how clearly the event demonstrated the gap in user experience between wallets that had it and those that did not.

supporting_evidence.quote: The core development team's primary post-incident action was not a protocol change but a recommendation: wallet developers without replace-by-fee support were strongly encouraged to add it, given how clearly the event demonstrated the gap in user experience between wallets that had it and those that did not.

claim: This recap restates the core motivation behind hybrid scoring for readers skimming only the executive summary of the specification.

supporting_evidence.quote: This recap restates the core motivation behind hybrid scoring for readers skimming only the executive summary of the specification.

claim: The precise mechanism by which age contributes to the score is described in full in the priority scoring formula section rather than repeated here.

supporting_evidence.quote: The precise mechanism by which age contributes to the score is described in full in the priority scoring formula section rather than repeated here.

claim: This recap restates the minimum relay fee figure for readers who only skim the DoS protections section of the specification.

supporting_evidence.quote: This recap restates the minimum relay fee figure for readers who only skim the DoS protections section of the specification.

claim: The figure has never changed since the mempool specification's original ratification, despite periodic community proposals to revisit it.

supporting_evidence.quote: The figure has never changed since the mempool specification's original ratification, despite periodic community proposals to revisit it.

claim: Transactions younger than one minute face no forced eviction at any accepted fee rate.

supporting_evidence.quote: | 0-1 min | Any accepted rate | No forced eviction |

claim: Transactions aged 1-24 hours must carry at least 4x the minimum relay fee and face forced eviction after 24 hours.

supporting_evidence.quote: | 1-24 hours | 4x minimum relay fee | 24 hours |

claim: At 32 peers, median transaction propagation is 0.9 seconds with a 99th percentile of 3.8 seconds.
At 128 peers, median propagation is 0.6 seconds with a 99th percentile of 2.4 seconds.
Helios computes transaction priority as score = alpha * (fee_rate / base_fee_rate) + (1 - alpha) * min(wait_seconds / max_wait_seconds, 1).

supporting_evidence.quote: score = alpha * (fee_rate / base_fee_rate) + (1 - alpha) * min(wait_seconds / max_wait_seconds, 1)

claim: A transaction is evicted on a new arrival only if it holds the lowest priority score among all mempool transactions.

supporting_evidence.quote: If a mempool holds k transactions and a new arrival ranks strictly above transaction i, then transaction i is evicted only if it currently holds the lowest priority score among all k transactions.

claim: Fee rates during the October 2022 congestion peaked at roughly nine times the trailing thirty-day median.

supporting_evidence.quote: Fee rates during the same period peaked at roughly nine times the network's trailing thirty-day median fee rate.

claim: Proposals to raise the minimum relay fee have been discussed periodically but never adopted.

supporting_evidence.quote: Proposals to raise the floor have been discussed periodically but never adopted, since the existing rate-limiting and dependency-tracking protections have proven sufficient on their own.

claim: Mempool acceptance requires the fee rate to meet or exceed the current minimum relay fee.

supporting_evidence.quote: 3. Check that the fee rate meets or exceeds the current minimum relay fee.

claim: A replacement transaction must spend the identical set of inputs as the original.

supporting_evidence.quote: 1. The replacement must spend the identical set of inputs as the original transaction.

claim: Nikolaidis's study of transaction propagation networks provides the gossip-latency model Helios's propagation section references.

supporting_evidence.quote: Nikolaidis's study of δίκτυα διάδοσης συναλλαγών (transaction propagation networks) provides the gossip-latency model referenced in the propagation section above.

claim: Chinese-language technical writing renders the fee concept as 手续费 regardless of the underlying fee mechanism.

supporting_evidence.quote: Chinese-language technical writing commonly renders the concept simply as 手续费 (handling fee) regardless of whether a first-price or auction-based mechanism is in use underneath.

claim: A condensed version of the postmortem appears in the network's operator onboarding materials as required reading.

supporting_evidence.quote: A condensed version of the postmortem now appears in the network's operator onboarding materials as required reading for anyone running a mempool-tracking node.
